title: Fishhawk operator role spec v0
description: Schema for the operator role spec (ADR-040 D1). The base role spec is a product artifact: the shipped default (docs/spec/operator-role-default.yaml) is versioned with the product and validated against this schema. Per-repo customization happens only through the thin overlay at .fishhawk/operator.yaml, validated against operator-role-overlay.schema.json. Breaking behavior changes bump to operator-role-v1; old role specs stay readable forever.

role.description: Role identity. v0 defines exactly one role; future roles get their own spec surfaces.

spec_version.description: Role spec version. Single-value enum per the docs/spec versioning rules; a breaking behavior change goes to operator-role-v1 in a new schema file.

mission.description: What the role is for, in prose. Defines behavior posture (distill, don't relay; act only within the workflow's operator_agent delegation), not authority — authority lives in the workflow spec's operator_agent knobs (ADR-040 D2).

gate_procedures.description: The playbook: ordered procedure steps per gate of the run lifecycle. The five keys are the closed v0 set. Procedure lives ONLY here — an overlay that tries to add or extend procedure fails the thinness rule.

escalation.description: When and how the role pages the human. Posture is fail-closed: anything not explicitly delegated pages.

always_page.description: Conditions that always page the human. Either a passthrough reference to the workflow spec's operator_agent.must_page_human list, or an explicit list of condition names.

page_format.description: The shape of a page: what the human receives instead of raw run state.

conventions.description: Named working conventions (snake_case key, prose value). This is the section a repo overlay merges into: local merge-ritual specifics and escalation contacts/channels belong here, never in gate_procedures.

forbidden.description: Actions the role must never take, regardless of delegation.

procedure.description: Ordered list of procedure steps; each step is a non-empty prose instruction.
